task_completion.description: Load the TitanicSurvival.csv dataset into Jamovi
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Jamovi window is open showing the TitanicSurvival dataset loaded, with columns such as 'age' and 'passengerClass' visible in the data view.

task_completion.description: Configure the One-Way ANOVA with correct variables
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Jamovi analysis setup panel for One-Way ANOVA, 'age' is placed in the 'Dependent Variables' box and 'passengerClass' is placed in the 'Grouping Variable' box.

task_completion.description: Select Welch's ANOVA, Levene's test, and Games-Howell post-hoc
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Jamovi interface, checkboxes for 'Don't assume equal (Welch's)', 'Homogeneity tests', and 'Games-Howell' are selected, OR the results pane explicitly displays tables for Welch's One-Way ANOVA, Homogeneity of Variances Test (Levene's), and Games-Howell Post-Hoc Comparisons.

task_completion.description: Save the Jamovi project as Titanic_Age_Analysis.omv
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi window title bar displays 'Titanic_Age_Analysis.omv', a save dialog shows this filename, or a file manager/terminal window shows the file exists in the ~/Documents/Jamovi/ directory.

task_completion.description: Create a text report documenting the specific statistical results
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output shows the contents of 'anova_report.txt'. The text must include Levene's p-value (< 0.05 or ~4.02e-06), Welch's F statistic (~99.97), Welch's p-value (< 0.001 or ~9.98e-38), and the Games-Howell post-hoc p-value for 1st vs 3rd class (< 0.001).

integrity.description: The agent used the Jamovi graphical interface to perform the analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Jamovi application being interacted with. The agent should not bypass the GUI by writing a Python/R script in the terminal to calculate the ANOVA.

integrity.description: The reported statistical values come from the actual Jamovi analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: The values typed into the text report should match the values visible in the Jamovi results pane in previous or current screenshots, proving the agent didn't just copy-paste expected answers from external knowledge.

integrity.description: The text report was actually saved to the specified path
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor should show the file name 'anova_report.txt' in its title/tab, or a terminal command like 'cat' or 'ls' should confirm the file exists at ~/Documents/Jamovi/anova_report.txt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Dataset path: /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/TitanicSurvival.csv. Project save path: /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/Titanic_Age_Analysis.omv. Report path: /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/anova_report.txt. Expected statistical results to look for in the Jamovi output and text report: Levene's p-value is < 0.05 (approx 4.02e-06), Welch's F statistic is approx 99.97, Welch's p-value is < 0.001 (approx 9.98e-38), and Games-Howell post-hoc p-value for 1st vs 3rd class is < 0.001.
